mainEntity.name: How does Global Changer support food manufacturers in their climate balance (Scope 1–3)?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the food industry, up to 90% of emissions often arise in Scope 3, primarily through raw materials, packaging, and supply chains. Global Changer structures Scope 1–3 according to the GHG Protocol. Particularly in Scope 3, the solution allows for the consistent recording of these emission sources. Emission factors are systematically assigned and versioned documented. The result is an audit-compliant and reproducible carbon footprint that transparently represents emissions across products, product groups, and locations.

mainEntity.name: How does Global Changer deal with complex Scope-3 supply chains in the food industry?

acceptedAnswer.text: Food products often consist of many ingredients and packaging components with different suppliers. Global Changer organizes these materials along Scope 3 categories and automatically consolidates emissions across products, commodity groups, and locations. As a result, manual Excel roll-ups, which lead to errors and inconsistencies in many companies, are eliminated. At the same time, it becomes clear which raw materials or supply chains have the greatest impact on the footprint.

mainEntity.name: How does Global Changer represent FLAG emissions and agricultural raw materials?

acceptedAnswer.text: Agricultural raw materials place special demands on emissions calculation, such as through biogenic emissions or changes in land use. Global Changer integrates industry-specific databases like AGRIBALYSE (also Agri-footprint in the future) and ecoinvent. This allows for a differentiated representation of agricultural raw materials instead of relying on generic average values. Companies thus receive a more realistic portrayal of their FLAG emissions.

mainEntity.name: How does Global Changer support the PCF calculation for food products?

acceptedAnswer.text: Product carbon footprints are often requested by retailers in the food industry. Global Changer structures AI-supported ingredients, packaging, processing, and transport along defined system boundaries according to ISO 14067 and ISO 14040/44 and automatically assigns suitable emission factors. This allows for the efficient creation of PCFs for individual products or entire portfolios, for example, for retailer inquiries or one's own sustainability reporting. The methodology and the PCF module of Global Changer are currently being reviewed and certified by an independent certification company.

mainEntity.name: How does Global Changer handle missing supplier data?

acceptedAnswer.text: In practice, primary data from suppliers is often only partially or not at all available. Global Changer bridges these gaps by automatically assigning emission factors from integrated databases such as ecoinvent or AGRIBALYSE. All AI-generated assumptions are marked as such and provided with confidence scores, making it clear where primary data is missing. Once supplier data is available, it can be directly integrated and replace previous assumptions. Companies can start with minimal data and gradually increase data depth without changing the methodological structure.

mainEntity.name: Why is ChatGPT or Copilot not enough to create PCFs for the food industry?

acceptedAnswer.text: Generative AI tools like ChatGPT or Copilot can research information, structure texts, or make rough assumptions. However, they are not sufficient for a reliable product carbon footprint (PCF) calculation. A verifiable PCF requires clearly defined system boundaries according to ISO 14067 and ISO 14040/44, consistent application of emission factors from integrated, versioned databases, reproducible calculation logic rather than one-time textual output, transparent documentation of all assumptions, and comparability across products, time periods, and locations. Generative AI works probabilistically. It generates answers based on probabilities, not on a structured, rule-based accounting system. In the food industry, this is particularly relevant: company-specific rules, such as the accounting for green electricity or the use of specific emission factors for agricultural raw materials like potatoes from Germany or for packaging materials like recycled aluminum, cannot be systematically recorded and automatically applied in generative AI tools. Global Changer combines AI-supported automation with firm methodological standards, integrated emission factors from databases like ecoinvent or AGRIBALYSE, rule-based logic, and traceable structure. This creates scalable, audit-ready PCFs rather than text-based approximations.

mainEntity.name: Is Global Changer suitable for CSRD and external audits?

acceptedAnswer.text: Global Changer supports the structured documentation of emissions in accordance with the GHG Protocol and facilitates preparation for regulatory requirements such as the CSRD. All calculations are thoroughly documented. This creates a solid foundation for internal analyses as well as external audits by auditors or inspectors. Auditors can access results and calculations directly in the software if needed – with defined viewing rights, without being able to intervene in the process. The methodology and the PCF module of Global Changer are currently being reviewed and certified by an independent certification company.
